title: phebalium maxwellii
intro: Phebalium maxwellii, also known as Maxwell's Baeckea, is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the Rutaceae family of plants. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Phebalium maxwellii include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Phebalium maxwellii can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
